Jehoram received warning by God’s judgment and removed the image of Baal, yet he continued the worship of the calves. Those do not truly repent or reform who only part with the sins they lose by, but continue to love the sins that they think to gain by.

The king of Israel lamented their distress and the danger they were in. He called these kings together, yet he attributed it to Providence. Thus, the foolishness of man distorts his way, and then his heart is troubled against the Lord. It was good that Jehoshaphat inquired of the Lord now, but it would have been much better if he had done it before he engaged in this war. Good men sometimes neglect their duty until necessity and affliction drive them to it. Wicked people often prosper from the friendship and society of the godly. To test their faith and obedience, Elisha instructed them to make the valley full of pits to receive water. Those who expect God’s blessings must dig pools for the rain to fill, as in the valley of Baca, and thus make even that a well. We need not inquire whence the water came. God is not limited by secondary causes. Those who sincerely seek for the dew of God’s grace will have it, and by it be made more than conquerors.

It is a blessing to be favored with the company of those who have power with God and can prevail through their prayers. A kingdom may be upheld and prosper because of the fervent prayers of those who are dear to God. May we place our highest regard upon such as are most precious in his account. When sinners say Peace, peace, destruction comes upon them; despair will follow their reckless presumption. In Satan’s service and at his suggestion, such horrible deeds have been done as cause the natural feelings of the heart to shudder, like the king of Moab sacrificing his son. It is well not to urge the worst of men to extremes; we should rather leave them to the judgment of God.
